hypothesis_en: Mr President, I was listening to Mr Rack with his magnificent report and I want to say that, as he pointed out in his presentation of this second reading, we are trying to improve road safety at an essential point that became apparent after the terrible accidents in the Alps in 1999 and in 2001. But it must be remembered that last week in the Swiss tunnel of Barek we had another fatal accident and, once again, the fires in the tunnels are proving to be one of the most dangerous elements for which there are answers. This proposal has been supported by Parliament, which has adopted several resolutions on this subject, and I would also like to say that it has been well received by the European Council and to recall that these proposals are also part of the initiatives taken by the United Nations in its European regional area on I would like to say above all that, although Parliament's will has always been enormously supportive, the same has been the case on the part of the Council, and that all this has enabled a full agreement between the three institutions after 15 months of discussions, and that therefore this text, which will be voted on by Parliament tomorrow, will be brought to light shortly. I would like to point out that the work of Parliament, which has carefully studied the Commission's proposal, which has tabled 78 extremely technical amendments after the first reading, shows its knowledge and dedication. I would like to thank Mr Rack and the Committee on Regional Policy, Transport and Tourism for having gone into the depths of this technical text and for having taken a constructive and effective approach throughout the procedure. Mr President, I would like to take this opportunity to thank the rapporteur, Mr Rack, but also Mr Markov, to whom I have not responded before, but I would like to congratulate him on his excellent report. I would like to ask the Commissioner whether he is aware of the fact that the Commission has already made a number of proposals to the Council and the European Parliament on the issue of driving time and safety, because only if we have good controls will we have a reality on the limits. Mr Cochilovo, as I said a moment ago, my congratulations on what you have achieved, and in general I would like to take this debate to thank all the members of the Committee on Transport and to thank them for the extraordinary cooperation that has been shown over the years. We have developed and enabled the adoption of a number of key measures to boost the transport sector in the European Union, to boost the transport sector, to make transport safer and more competitive, to make the internal market a reality, to respect the environment and to make sustainable development a reality, with transport in the service of the growth and well-being of European citizens. I would like to talk very briefly about the adoption of the first railway package, tomorrow we will discuss the second railway package which finally allows the establishment of a European railway, the adoption and implementation of a single European sky which will make the modernisation of the management of our European space a reality, integrating the railway sector into our airspace. The adoption of all the maritime safety measures we have discussed this morning, the push and the implementation and the final launch of the Galileo project, which makes Europe a technological leader capable of competing at all levels in the area of the greatest technological development in the space sector, I would also like to mention some issues, such as support for passenger rights, the promotion of security measures, both in the sense of security and in the sense of surety. I am referring to the air, maritime and rail sectors, the protection of the environment through the creation of operational agencies for all modes of transport, the promotion of social legislation and many other proposals adopted, not to mention international agreements in sectors such as the maritime sector, the air sector and the promotion of cooperation with all our neighbours, both in the Mediterranean area and in the Eurasian continent. Mr President, I did not want, despite the movement and the noise, to stop thanking you for all the work we have done together and to say that I think we can be reasonably satisfied because during this time we have not only not lost time, but we have contributed to the construction of a more balanced, better developed Europe at the service of the citizens and also with a global projection.
reference_en: now I was listening to Mr Rack he's produced a magnificent report and I'd just like to say that as he pointed out (1.32) as he was giving his presentation on the second reading process what we're trying to do is to improve the level of safety on our roads in a key point which became clear following the terrible accidents in the Alps in nineteen ninety and nine and two thousand and one but we have to recall that last week in the Baregg tunnel in Switzerland we had another fatal accident and once again fires in tunnels show that this is one of the most dangerous risks and that we have to be able to deal with these we have to if we can't avoid such fires we have to reduce their consequences to the minimum and in two thousand and two the Commission came up with a draft directive with minimum safety requirements for tunnels that proposal was supported by the Parliament which adopted various resolutions on the subject and I'd also like to say that it was welcomed by Council as well and also to recall that these proposals are to be seen within the context of the initiatives put forward by the United Nations (1.12) as part of its European regional area for transport and I would also like to say that without Parliament's cooperation and support and indeed Council's cooperation and support it wouldn't have been possible for us to conclude fifteen months of ne= of negotiations with full agreement between the three nego= Institutions which means that this text that will be voted on in Parliament tomorrow will have the green light accorded to it in as the (1.5) very short term I'd just like to say that Parliament has looked in great detail at the Commission's work and has put forward hundreds of very technical amendments as of the first reading Parliament has shown just how familiar it is with the subject and how dedicated it is to it most of these amendments have in fact been incorporated in the amended proposal put forward by the Commission and the Council adopted the same approach on the twenty-sixth of February last I'd like to thank Mr Rack I'd like to thank the regional policy regional affairs committee too for having grappled so brilliantly with this highly technical text and for having always approached it constructively throughout the whole procedure safety in tunnels in the states of the Union will in future (1.18) be (2.15) something where we can act positively to raise safety levels and all of the states of the Union will hopefully thus be able to improve safety not just on the roads but also at the vulnerable points of the system tunnels President I'd like to take this opportunity to thank once more the rapporteur Mr Rack but also Mr Markov (1.54) I didn't answer his points earlier but I did want to congratulate him for the work that he achieved on the subject of driving periods and safety issues and checks because it's only if we have proper effective checks that we can really ensure limits to driving time we won't make progress otherwise digital tachographs you know the efforts that we've made and in fact I'll be sending you a letter in short term on the decisions that we're taking there and there'll be a decision in July and in fact they're going to be installed as of July in certain countries Mr Cocilovo as I said a few moments ago I wanted to welcome I wanted to congratulate him on what he's achieved and ladies and gentlemen I wanted to take advantage of this debate to thank all of the members of the Regional Affairs Committee and to thank them for the extraordinary level of cooperation that we've managed to enjoy over these years which has made it possible for us to adopt together a series of key measures to encourage the growth of the transport sector in the European Union to ensure that our transport system is safer is more competitive is in line with internal market requirements as well as requirements for environmental sustainability and the prosperity and well-being of our citizens very briefly I would perhaps recall the adoption of the first railway package tomorrow the second railway package will be discussed whereby finally we'll be able to establish a European railways network the adoption of the establishment of a single European sky which will mean that the modernisation of the management of the European airspace will become a reality integrating as we've done with the rail sector (1.09) our (1.04) skies into a single area a European area the adoption of all of the maritime safety measures which we discussed (1.43) this morning the encouragement and implementation and final launch of the Galileo project which turns Europe into a technological pioneer capable of competing at all levels of major space technological development the revision of the trans-European networks which following our common work I hope will be finally adopted the day after tomorrow all of these subjects I think have been sybol= symbolic there are some other points that I wouldn't like to neglect such as the support for passengers rights encouragement of safety rules safety and security both in the air transport sector the rail sector and the protection ~oof all (1.41) forms of transport and encouragement of social legislation and other proposals that have been adopted as well as the international agreements that have been struck in the maritime sector the air sector and the encouragement of cooperation with all of our neighbours both in the Mediterranean and with other continents President (1.04) in spite of the noise I (1.23) would like to thank you for all of the work that we've done together I think all of us can be reasonably satisfied because over this period of time not only have we not lost time but I think we have contributed to building up a more balanced better developed Europe more at the service of its citizens and with a higher international profile
source_es: señor Presidente escuchaba al Señor Rack con su magnífico informe y quiero decir que como ha señalado él en esta= al hilo de su presentación de esta segunda lectura estamos (1.26) intentando mejorar la seguridad en nuestras carreteras en un punto esencial que quedó de manifiesto después de los terribles accidentes en los Alpes en el año noventa y nueve y en el año dos mil uno pero hay que recordar que la semana pasada en el túnel suizo de Baregg de nuevo tuvimos un accidente mortal y una vez más los incendios en los túneles se demuestran como uno de los elementos más peligrosos sobre los cuales hay que tener para los cuales hay que tener respuestas para evitar este tipo de accidentes o al menos reducir al mínimo sus consecuencias la Comisión envió en el año dos mil dos la propuesta de directiva por la que se establece una serie de requisitos mínimos de seguridad para los túneles esta propuesta ha recibido el apoyo del Parlamento que había aprobado varias resoluciones sobre este tema y también quiere quiero decir que ha habido una buena acogida por parte del Consejo Europeo y recordar además que estas propuestas se enmarcan dentro de lo que son también las iniciativas aportadas en el ámbito de Naciones Unidas en su área regional europea relativa al sector del transporte y quiero sobre todo decir que si el= la voluntad del Parlame= del Parlamento ha sido siempre de enorme apoyo lo mismo ha sido por parte del Consejo y que todo ello ha permitido que por fin tras quince meses de discusiones haya un pleno acuerdo entre los tres las tres instituciones y que por lo tanto este texto que se va a votar mañana por parte del Parlamento verá la luz en breve (1.07) quiero señalar que el trabajo del Parlamento que ha estudiado minuciosamente la propuesta de la Comisión que ha presentado setenta y ocho enmiendas enormemente técnicas después de la primera lectura muestra su conocimiento y su dedicación la mayor parte de las mismas han sido recogidas en la propuesta modificada que presentó la Comisión y lo mismo hizo el Consejo en su posición común del veintiséis de febrero pasado quiero agradecer al Señor Rack y a la Comisión po= de Política Regional Transportes y Turismo que se haya metido a fondo con este texto tan técnico y que se haya planteado con un enfoque siempre constructivo y eficaz a lo largo de todo el procedimiento los ingenieros responsables de la seguridad de los túneles de los Estados de la Unión tienen de ahora en adelante ante sí una larga tarea para poner en práctica en todos los túneles de la Unión y también por lo tanto los Estados de la Unión hacer un esfuerzo que permita mejorar la seguridad en nuestras carreteras en los ámbitos en estos puntos vulnerables que son los túneles señor Presidente quiero aprovechar muy brevemente para dar las gracias como he dicho al ponente Señor Rack pero también al Señor Markov (1.08) que no= al cual no he contestado antes pero quiero felicitar por el trabajo realizado en cuanto a la cuestión del tiempo de conducción y en cuanto a la cuestión de seguridad y los controles porque solo si tenemos buenos controles tendremos una realidad en cuanto a la= los límites si no hay controles podemos decir lo que queramos no se llevarán a cabo los tacógrafos digitales sabe su Señoría lo que el esfuerzo que hemos hecho recibirá una carta en breve al respecto señalándole las decisiones que hemos determinado y que permitirán que a partir de los próximos d= ~deel del próximo mes de julio ya se empiece a instalar en algunos países ese tipo de tacógrafos al Señor Cocilovo también como le decía hace un momento mi felicitación por lo que ha conseguido y en general quiero aprovechar Señorías ~estaa este debate para dar las gracias a todos los miembros de la Comisión de Transportes y agradecerles la cooperación extraordinaria que a lo largo de estos años hemos desarrollado y que ha permitido adop= la adopción de una serie de medidas claves para el impulso del sector del transporte en la Unión Europea para el impulso del sector del transporte un transporte más seguro más competitivo la realidad del mercado interior la realidad del respeto del medio ambiente y la realidad de una desarrollo duradero con un transporte al servicio del crecimiento y del bienestar de los ciudadanos europeos yo recordaría muy brevemente la adopción del primer paquete ferroviario mañana se ma= discutirá el segundo paquete ferroviario que permite el establecimiento por fin de un ferrocarril europeo la adopción y la puesta en práctica de un cielo único europeo que va a convertir en realidad la modernización de la gestión de nuestro espacio europeo integrando lo mismo en el sector del ferroviario nuestro nuestro espacio y haciéndolo haciendo el espacio aéreo y el espacio ferroviario un espacio europeo la adopción de todas las medidas de seguridad marítima a las cuales nos hemos referido hace hemos dis= que sobre las cuales hemos discutido a lo largo de esta mañana el impulso y la realización y el lanzamiento definitivo del proyecto Galileo que convierte a Europa en un líder tecnológico capaz de convertir de competir a todos los niveles en el área de mayor desarrollo tecnológico en el sector del espacio la revisión de las redes transeuropeas que tras nuestro colosal trabajo en común espero se adopten definitivamente pasado mañana y si estos temas han sido emblemáticos tampoco quiero olvidar algunos asuntos como por ejemplo lo que es la debida la= el apoyo a los derechos de los pasajeros el impulso a las medidas de seguridad tanto en el sentido de sécurité como en el sentido de sûreté tanto en el sector aéreo como marítimo como ferroviario la protección de a través de la creación de agencias operativas de todos los modos de transporte e la el impulso de la legislación social y tantas otras propuestas adoptadas por no hablar del acuerdo los acuerdos internacionales en sectores como el sector marítimo el sector aéreo y el impulso de la cooperación con todos nuestros vecinos tanto en el área mediterránea como en el área del ~conteniente euroasiático yo señor Presidente no quería a pesar del movimiento y de los ruidos que hay dejar de darles las gracias por todo el trabajo realizado conjuntamente y decir que creo que podemos estar razonablemente satisfechos porque durante este tiempo no sólo no hemos perdido el tiempo sino que hemos contribuido a la construcción de una Europa más equilibrada mejor desarrollada al servicio de los ciudadanos y también con una proyección mundial muchas gracias señor Presidente
